title: CACOON - 211info
text: GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 528 E Main Street Suite E John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 575-0429 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 575-0429 Main phone Main phone (541) 575-0429 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Grant County Services: Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home Based Parenting Education * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - VITAL RECORDS GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT RURAL HEALTH CLINIC HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - STD CLINIC HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - PUBLIC HEALTH MODERNIZATION HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INFORMATION SERVICES - TOBACCO PREVENTION AND EDUCATION PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
